channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: IAF Plans Dedicated S-400 Squadron for NCR Protection Under “Sudarshan Chakra” Missile Shield
item.description: The Indian Air Force is moving toward a far more expansive and layered air defence architecture with plans to evolve its existing S-400 deployment into a 10-squadron “Sudarshan Chakra” missile shield, with one elite squadron earmarked specifically for the protection of the National Capital Region (NCR) while also serving as a mobile strategic reserve capable of rapid redeployment across both western and northern fronts.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: GTRE Confirms FUFA Stealth UCAV Program to Take Priority Over RSPA UCAV Development
item.description: India’s push toward next-generation unmanned combat aviation is accelerating, with fresh confirmations indicating that the Futuristic Unmanned Fighter Aircraft (FUFA) program has now taken priority over the Remotely Piloted Strike Aircraft (RSPA). The development marks a significant shift in focus toward high-speed, stealthy unmanned systems that align closely with emerging sixth-generation air combat doctrines.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: HAL’s Showcases updated RUAV-200 Unmanned Helicopter
item.description: India’s push toward battlefield autonomy and indigenous unmanned systems received a notable boost as the Indian Army Chief was presented with the RUAV-200, a rotary unmanned aerial vehicle developed by Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL). The platform, designed for both the Indian Army and Navy, reflects a significant maturation in India’s rotary UAV segment, with visible refinements indicating it is approaching limited series production readiness.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: US Signals ‘Open Pathway’ for India to Acquire F-35 Stealth Fighters After Pentagon Talks with Foreign Secretary
item.description: In a development that could have far-reaching implications for India’s future airpower strategy, the United States has indicated that a potential pathway remains open for India to acquire the F-35 fifth-generation stealth fighter. The signal came during a high-level meeting at the Pentagon on April 9, 2026, where Mike Duffey, U.S. Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment, held discussions with India’s Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri, who is on a three-day official visit to Washington.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army Plans Massive Induction of Indigenous Drones and Loitering Munitions to Transform Future Warfare Capability
item.description: The Indian Army is preparing for a large-scale transformation of its battlefield capabilities through the planned induction of tens of thousands of domestically developed unmanned aerial systems (UAS) and loitering munitions over the next five years. This move reflects a decisive doctrinal shift driven by the growing influence of unmanned systems in modern warfare, as demonstrated in ongoing conflicts across regions such as West Asia.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Finalises Three-Theatre Military Command Structure for China, Pakistan and Maritime Fronts, Vice CDS Role Planned
item.description: India has moved closer to one of its most consequential military reforms in decades, with the broad contours of theaterisation now largely finalised. The proposed structure envisions three integrated theatre commands aligned to India’s primary security challenges—China, Pakistan, and the maritime domain—marking a decisive shift toward joint warfighting and unified command architecture.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Why Fancy Fighters and Rockets Alone Won’t Save India in the Next Border Crisis
item.description: In recent debates shaping India’s defence discourse, a former Navy admiral has suggested that high-end fighter jets may be relics of a bygone era, while a former Indian Air Force leader has questioned the necessity of dedicated rocket forces in the Indian context. These views spark important conversations about resource allocation and technological priorities. Yet, the perspective that truly grounds these discussions comes from the experience of the humble infantryman, who understands the unforgiving reality of ground warfare along contested borders.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army’s AH-64E Apache Attack Helicopter firing a AGM-114 Hellfire in Exercise Brahmastra at the Pokhran Firing Range
item.description: The Indian Army showcased the combat capability of its Boeing AH-64 Apache attack helicopters during the live firing exercise ‘Brahmastra’ at the Pokhran Firing Range in Rajasthan on April 9. As part of the exercise, the Apaches executed precision strikes using AGM-114 Hellfire missiles, rockets, and onboard gun systems, successfully engaging designated targets in a simulated battlefield setting. The drill aimed to assess both operational readiness and coordination between pilots and ground support teams.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Multi-domain operations a present imperative for India: Air Marshal Dixit
item.description: Chief of Integrated Defence Staff Air Marshal Ashutosh Dixit on Thursday said India’s environment makes the transformation towards Multi-Domain Operations (MDO) urgent, as the country faces threats that do not respect domain boundaries.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Op Sindoor showed India’s progression towards ‘domain jointness: Army chief
item.description: Army Chief General Upendra Dwivedi on Thursday said Operation Sindoor demonstrated India’s progression towards “domain jointness” and called the military offensive a “defining case study” of operational significance of integration.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: “Impressive”: Nuke Watchdog Praises India’s Nuclear Milestone At Kalpakkam
item.description: Rafael Mariano Grossi, the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Director General, has warmly appreciated India’s achievement in attaining criticality of the Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor at Kalpakkam in Tamil Nadu.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India finalising deal to supply oil, gas to Mauritius: EAM Jaishankar
item.description: India is finalising an agreement for the supply of oil and gas to Mauritius to strengthen its energy security amid the crisis due to the West Asia conflict, External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar said here on Thursday. Jaishankar, who is in Mauritius to attend the 9th Indian Ocean Conference, said the ongoing crisis in West Asia as Mauritius Prime Minister Navin Ramgoolam reminded him, has underscored the importance of strategic partnerships, especially in the energy sector.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Israel denounces Pak Defence Minister Khwaja Asif for ‘outrageous’ comments
item.description: In a strong denouncement of “outrageous” remarks made by Pakistan Defence Minister Khawaja Asif, Israel has raised doubts about his country’s role as a “neutral arbiter”. “Pakistan Defence Minister’s call for Israel’s annihilation is outrageous,” the Israeli Prime Minister’s Office said in a post on X. “This is not a statement that can be tolerated from any government, especially not from one that claims to be a neutral arbiter for peace,” the PMO said Thursday evening.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Israel Encouraged By India’s Support For Free Maritime Navigation: Envoy Azar
item.description: Spotlighting that India continues to pursue its national interests strongly amid the ongoing geopolitical conflicts, Reuven Azar, the Ambassador of Israel to India, on Thursday lauded New Delhi’s stance on unimpeded freedom of navigation as efforts continue to resume the use of commercial maritime routes in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India And Sweden Agree To Strengthen Ties In Trade, Defence And Innovation
item.description: India and Sweden held the eighth round of Foreign Office Consultations (FOCs) in New Delhi on Thursday, agreeing to strengthen cooperation in political engagements, trade and investment, defence and security, innovation, sustainability and people-to-people ties.
item.dc:creator: admin
